XM.src: RefSeq NM_001446166.1; genomic exon model confirmed vs NCBI XM_047444434.1 (byte-identical mRNA, full md5 882366312f718378c8c6aed8d616b2dc), GRCh38 RS_2025_08 placement on NC_000002.12, all 4 boundaries exact; NM_001446166.1 (13-MAR-2026) postdates RS_2025_08 so is not itself in the released annotation

ms.acc_alias_note: XP_047300390.1 (Table B / the OpenProt join) and NP_001280157.1 (RefSeq) are ALIASES of the SAME protein — OpenProt lists them on one record. This is a DATABASE-NAMESPACE difference, not a conflict. The viewer cites Table B's namespace so the two agree.

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 3 unique peptides (OpenProt XP_047300390.1). ATTRIBUTION NOT ESTABLISHED BY MS, AND NOT ESTABLISHABLE: 18 same-stop ORFs in the 763-ORF catalogue CONTAIN N-Myc's entire sequence (ATG-001 at 593 aa, ATG-002 at 575 aa, and 16 near-cognate extensions), so NO peptide of N-Myc can EVER exclude them — this holds whatever the three peptides are. N-Myc's identity rests on ANNOTATION, not on these peptides; annotation is not MS evidence. What the MS establishes is that A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED.

ms.acc_alias_note: ENSP00000491476.1 (Table B / the OpenProt join) and NP_001280160.1 (RefSeq) are ALIASES of the SAME protein — OpenProt lists them on one record. This is a DATABASE-NAMESPACE difference, not a conflict. The viewer cites Table B's namespace so the two agree.

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 4 unique peptides (OpenProt ENSP00000491476.1). ATTRIBUTION IS IMPOSSIBLE IN PRINCIPLE, PROVEN BY IN-SILICO TRYPTIC DIGEST (dmycn_digest.py; no OpenProt peptide retrieval required). dMYCN's 253 aa = a 52-aa N-terminal leader SHARED VERBATIM with MYCNOT, MUSEP and ORF 11, plus a 201-aa C-terminal block SHARED VERBATIM with N-Myc. 52 + 201 = 253: dMYCN HAS ZERO RESIDUES OF ITS OWN. Only a peptide SPANNING the 52|53 junction could discriminate it; of its 41 MS-detectable tryptic peptides, ZERO are unique. The shortest junction-spanning peptide is 33 aa (AESPPDPLQSAGDDEDDEEEDEEEEIDVVTVEK, residues 41-73) — PAST the 30-aa detectability ceiling — and all six junction peptides are in any case SHARED with NC-EXT-033 and NC-NEST-013/-014/-015. Impossible on TWO INDEPENDENT GROUNDS, robust across windows 7-30 through 7-50 aa and 2 missed cleavages. OpenProt's 4 peptides therefore CANNOT be discriminating peptides: they must lie in the shared leader or the shared C-terminal block. A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED; WHICH START PRODUCED IT IS NOT ESTABLISHED.

9.location: 5'-leader overlapping uORF (extends into N-Myc CDS, out-of-frame)

ms.role: control — MS axis proven live at this locus (7 peptides)
ms.peptides_note: individual peptide sequences NOT RECORDED (not retrieved from OpenProt)

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 7 unique peptides (OpenProt NP_001280162.1/Q9H224). MYCNOT IS THE LIVE POSITIVE CONTROL: it proves the MS ASSAY FIRES at this locus — a short regulatory ORF at MYCN IS detectable by lysate MS — and it is what makes the THREE genuine MS negatives (MUSEP, ATG-015, ATG-022) informative rather than under-powered. IT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE ITS OWN START: MYCNOT shares its stop with 23 other ORFs in the 763-ORF catalogue, and NC-EXT-035 (CTG, 119 aa) CONTAINS MYCNOT IN ITS ENTIRETY — its 7 peptides cannot exclude it. THE CONTROL'S SCOPE IS THE ASSAY, NOT ATTRIBUTION — INCLUDING ITS OWN. It licenses the claim 'a protein from this stop group is translated'; it licenses NOTHING about which start produced it, and it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE.

ms.role: novel ORF — first in the 763 with direct protein evidence

24.source: OpenProt 2.2 IP_083082 (MS-detected, 2 peptides); sequence GENCODE v49 (GRCh38.p14)

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — THE PEPTIDES DO NOT DISCRIMINATE THE START. OpenProt IP_083082 reports 2 unique peptides (SGVPPPTPR, LSPHSPSLCVPR) at the OpenProt reporting floor, across 3 lysate datasets. THE TWO PEPTIDES ARE CONTAINED IN 6 OF THE 8 sORFs.org-DETECTED REGION-1 MEMBERS, AND IN 16 ORFs ACROSS THE FULL 763-ORF CATALOGUE (2 ATC/ATT extensions LARGER than ATG-016, the 5 ATG starts, and near-cognates down to NC-NEST-092; 2 further ORFs contain one peptide). THE RIBO-SEQ CLUSTER (8) AND THE MS CANDIDATE SET (16) ARE DIFFERENT SETS WITH DIFFERENT DENOMINATORS — records != events, on two axes, with two bases. Any of the 16 would yield exactly these two peptides. The peptides EXCLUDE NC-NEST-128 (contains neither) and cannot be explained by NC-NEST-108 alone (contains only LSPHSPSLCVPR). The other starts are NOT catalogued by OpenProt and were NEVER SEARCHED — NOT-ASSESSED, not ABSENT. A PROTEIN IS TRANSLATED FROM THIS STOP GROUP; WHICH START PRODUCED IT IS NOT ESTABLISHED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START — the uniqueness search (uniqueness_digest.py, b0514f64) compared against FOUR hard-coded proteins plus UniProt, a database that does NOT contain the nested competitors. The peptides ARE frame-disjoint from N-Myc/dMYCN, so they are not fragments of the canonical protein. Detection is not function; and here, detection is not attribution.

sorfs.text: DETECTED (1,540 reads) — NESTED START of Region 1. NOT an independent detection. 8 nested starts share stop 15,945,657 and ONE read pile. OUT-OF-FRAME PHASING (count_0/1/2 = 61/152/840; orfscore -10.77; in_frame No): periodicity is in frame 2, consistent with the HOST N-Myc CDS, NOT this ORF's own frame. Occupancy at the locus, NOT own-frame translational evidence. Single study (werner_2015).

cluster.text: Region 1 (15,945,493-15,945,657) — members: ORFs 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 49, 50, 51 (ATG-016/017/018/019/020 + NC-NEST-092/-108/-128). ALL EIGHT SHARE STOP 15,945,657 AND ONE READ PILE = ONE ORF, NOT EIGHT. The cluster is COMPLETE in this table (all 8 members present). Records != events.

Its sequence CONTAINS BOTH peptides of the Region-1 detection (SGVPPPTPR, LSPHSPSLCVPR) — it is one of the CANDIDATE SOURCES; the peptides cannot exclude it.

ms_lysate.text: NOT-ASSESSED — NOT catalogued by OpenProt (no accession): this ORF was never in the MS search space, so it was NEVER SEARCHED. NOT a negative. The MYCNOT 7-peptide control proves the ASSAY FIRES; it does not extend the SEARCH SPACE. Its sequence CONTAINS BOTH peptides of the Region-1 detection (SGVPPPTPR, LSPHSPSLCVPR) — it is one of the CANDIDATE SOURCES; the peptides cannot exclude it.

sorfs.text: DETECTED (1,534 reads) — NESTED START of Region 1. NOT an independent detection. 8 nested starts share stop 15,945,657 and ONE read pile. OUT-OF-FRAME PHASING (count_0/1/2 = 61/152/840; orfscore -10.77; in_frame No): periodicity is in frame 2, consistent with the HOST N-Myc CDS, NOT this ORF's own frame. Occupancy at the locus, NOT own-frame translational evidence. Single study (werner_2015).

cluster.text: Region 1 (15,945,493-15,945,657) — members: ORFs 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 49, 50, 51 (ATG-016/017/018/019/020 + NC-NEST-092/-108/-128). ALL EIGHT SHARE STOP 15,945,657 AND ONE READ PILE = ONE ORF, NOT EIGHT. The cluster is COMPLETE in this table (all 8 members present). Records != events.

evidence_resources.label: MS-lysate (OpenProt 2.2)
evidence_resources.caveat: READ THE TWO LIMITS OF THIS AXIS BEFORE READING ANY CELL. (1) COVERAGE: the MYCNOT 7-peptide control proves the ASSAY FIRES at this locus; it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE. OpenProt catalogues only 9 of the 763 ORFs; every ORF without an accession was NEVER IN THE SEARCH SPACE and so was NEVER SEARCHED = NOT-ASSESSED, NOT a negative. The ONLY real MS negatives are the 3 ORFs that ARE catalogued and returned 0 peptides. (2) ATTRIBUTION: NO ORF AT THIS LOCUS IS START-DISCRIMINATED BY LYSATE MS. The locus is a dense nest of same-stop ORFs, and EVERY MS-detected ORF has a same-stop host that contains it in its entirety (N-Myc 18 hosts, MUSEP 5, ATG-016 2, dMYCN 1, MYCNOT 1 — the control included), so no peptide of it can ever exclude that host. Peptide-level START attribution is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE here — everywhere, not only in Region 1. THE NAMED PROTEINS ARE IDENTIFIED BY ANNOTATION, NOT BY MS. THE HONEST CEILING OF THIS AXIS IS: 'A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED' — FOR ALL FOUR DETECTIONS, N-MYC INCLUDED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START.)
evidence_resources.header_verbatim: MS-lysate: OpenProt 2.2 — READ THE TWO LIMITS OF THIS AXIS BEFORE READING ANY CELL. (1) COVERAGE: the MYCNOT 7-peptide control proves the ASSAY FIRES at this locus; it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE. OpenProt catalogues only 9 of the 763 ORFs; every ORF without an accession was NEVER IN THE SEARCH SPACE and so was NEVER SEARCHED = NOT-ASSESSED, NOT a negative. The ONLY real MS negatives are the 3 ORFs that ARE catalogued and returned 0 peptides. (2) ATTRIBUTION: NO ORF AT THIS LOCUS IS START-DISCRIMINATED BY LYSATE MS. The locus is a dense nest of same-stop ORFs, and EVERY MS-detected ORF has a same-stop host that contains it in its entirety (N-Myc 18 hosts, MUSEP 5, ATG-016 2, dMYCN 1, MYCNOT 1 — the control included), so no peptide of it can ever exclude that host. Peptide-level START attribution is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE here — everywhere, not only in Region 1. THE NAMED PROTEINS ARE IDENTIFIED BY ANNOTATION, NOT BY MS. THE HONEST CEILING OF THIS AXIS IS: 'A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED' — FOR ALL FOUR DETECTIONS, N-MYC INCLUDED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START.)

ms_axis_scope: MS-lysate: OpenProt 2.2 — READ THE TWO LIMITS OF THIS AXIS BEFORE READING ANY CELL. (1) COVERAGE: the MYCNOT 7-peptide control proves the ASSAY FIRES at this locus; it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE. OpenProt catalogues only 9 of the 763 ORFs; every ORF without an accession was NEVER IN THE SEARCH SPACE and so was NEVER SEARCHED = NOT-ASSESSED, NOT a negative. The ONLY real MS negatives are the 3 ORFs that ARE catalogued and returned 0 peptides. (2) ATTRIBUTION: NO ORF AT THIS LOCUS IS START-DISCRIMINATED BY LYSATE MS. The locus is a dense nest of same-stop ORFs, and EVERY MS-detected ORF has a same-stop host that contains it in its entirety (N-Myc 18 hosts, MUSEP 5, ATG-016 2, dMYCN 1, MYCNOT 1 — the control included), so no peptide of it can ever exclude that host. Peptide-level START attribution is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE here — everywhere, not only in Region 1. THE NAMED PROTEINS ARE IDENTIFIED BY ANNOTATION, NOT BY MS. THE HONEST CEILING OF THIS AXIS IS: 'A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED' — FOR ALL FOUR DETECTIONS, N-MYC INCLUDED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START.)
uniqueness_bound: Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation. It CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START. The two peptides are unique across the human reference proteome (UniProt UP000005640, 147,506 sequences) — but five of the six Region-1 candidates are ABSENT FROM EVERY REFERENCE PROTEOME, so the uniqueness test ran against a database that DID NOT CONTAIN THE COMPETITORS. It was never capable of discriminating them. (Preserved: uniqueness_digest.py, md5 b0514f64, lines 10–18 — the comparison set was FOUR named proteins plus UniProt, never the Region-1 siblings. Demonstrated, not inferred.)
